response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of an open-source Geo-Data platform, GIS work for Green Map, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems work in DLang handling millions of requests per second with async I/O and caching. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ or Rust, and his C/C++ experience is brief and dated (2014–2015), with recent years focused on TypeScript/Node full-stack product work. He has no direct routing or navigation-systems experience, so an interview would need to probe whether his systems-programming depth transfers to a C++ routing tile pipeline.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS experience, including founding and operating an open-source geo-data platform
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang, async I/O, fibers, microservices at millions of req/s)
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and Linux/Kubernetes operational experience
concerns: Modern C++ experience is limited and nearly a decade old; no Rust experience listed
No direct experience with routing engines, navigation guidance, or map-data tiling pipelines
Recent roles (2020–present) center on TypeScript/Node web product work, away from systems programming
